verified.notes: Kentucky PACE is an active program as of April 2026. Age requirement is 55+ for seniors. No income or asset limits found in official sources; PACE eligibility is based on clinical need (nursing facility level of care) and ability to live safely in community with services. Phone number is for Senior CommUnity Care of Kentucky PACE (Jefferson County). Additional PACE organizations may operate in other Kentucky counties with different contact numbers.

programs.programName: Kentucky Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)

verified.notes: Income limits from Kentucky state agency PDF (KHBE) for QMB program as of 2026. Asset limits from Kentucky DB101 (state-level benefits database). Phone number not found in provided search results; recommend contacting Kentucky Cabinet for Health and Family Services (CHFS) Division of Medicaid Services directly. No age restriction applies to these programs—eligibility is based on Medicare enrollment, income, and assets only.

verified.notes: SNAP has no minimum age threshold for seniors; households with members 60+ may qualify via net income/asset tests if over gross income limit. Income limits are gross for Oct 2025-Sept 2026. No specific asset limits detailed for seniors; general asset test applies with exemptions. snapscreener.com cites KY DCBS.

verified.notes: Age 65+ is the senior-specific threshold for HCB Waiver eligibility; program also serves disabled adults up to age 64. Income limit of $2,982/month (2026) applies to applicants regardless of marital status. For married couples with both spouses as applicants, each spouse can retain $2,000 in assets individually. Phone number (877) 315-0589 is for HCB program information from Department for Aging and Independent Living (DAIL); alternative application line is (855) 306-8959 for initial Medicaid eligibility through DCBS.

Income limit of $2,982/month (2026) applies to applicants regardless of marital status. For married couples with both spouses as applicants, each spouse can retain $2,000 in assets individually. Phone number (877) 315-0589 is for HCB program information from Department for Aging and Independent Living (DAIL); alternative application line is (855) 306-8959 for initial Medicaid eligibility through DCBS.

verified.notes: Kentucky operates the Nutrition Program for the Elderly statewide through Area Agencies on Aging and Independent Living. No income or asset limits found in official sources. Phone number is for KIPDA home-delivered meal referrals; congregate meal access varies by county with separate contact numbers. Some areas have waiting lists.
